_comment: Arlington County Employees' Retirement System (ACERS). Fully independent defined benefit pension system (not VRS). Covers county employees under three code chapters: Ch.21 (pre-1969 general/uniform), Ch.35 (school board, 1969+), Ch.46 (all hired 2/8/1981+). $3.28B assets, 111% funded (FY2024). Update as Arlington County Code changes.

jurisdiction.note: ACERS is a fully independent single-employer defined benefit pension system established 1953 under authority of the Virginia General Assembly. It is NOT part of VRS. Governed by Chapters 21, 35, and 46 of the Arlington County Code. A formal trust was adopted in December 2001.

hireDateMapping.label: ACERS Chapter 21 (Legacy General/Uniform)
hireDateMapping.note: Pre-1969 general and uniform employees — legacy chapter. Very few if any active members remain.

hireDateMapping.label: ACERS Chapter 35 (School Board)
hireDateMapping.note: School Board employees hired 1/1/1969 through 2/7/1981. Covered under Chapter 35.

hireDateMapping.label: ACERS Chapter 46 General (Pre-2009)
hireDateMapping.note: General members hired 2/8/1981 through 1/3/2009. Chapter 46, original 1.5% formula.

hireDateMapping.label: ACERS Chapter 46 General (Post-2009)
hireDateMapping.note: General members hired 1/4/2009 through 1/11/2025. Irrevocable election between benefit options at retirement.

hireDateMapping.label: ACERS Chapter 46 General (Post-2025)
hireDateMapping.note: General members hired 1/12/2025 or later. New tier provisions per Ord. No. 24-13.

hireDateMapping.label: ACERS Chapter 46 Public Safety
hireDateMapping.note: Police officers, firefighters, deputy sheriffs hired 2/8/1981+. Chapter 46 public safety provisions.

formula.description: Legacy formula — pre-1969 hires. Very few if any active members remain. User enters estimated monthly benefit.

acers_ch21.note: Chapter 21 members have legacy provisions. Consumer applications typically accept a user-provided monthly estimate.

cola.description: Varies by chapter — consult Arlington County Code

formula.description: 0.625% × Average Final Compensation (highest 36 consecutive months) × Years of Creditable Service

eligibility.note: Consult Chapter 35 provisions — generally mirrors Chapter 21 eligibility

contributions.note: Contribution rates set by Chapter 35 provisions

cola.description: Varies — consult Chapter 35 of Arlington County Code

acers_ch35.note: School Board employees hired 1/1/1969 through 2/7/1981. Supplemental to VRS for covered positions.

formula.description: 1.5% × Average Final Compensation × Years of Creditable Service (max 30 years). AFC = average of 3 highest 12 consecutive months (26 consecutive pay periods) using creditable compensation as defined prior to 1/4/2009.

eligibility.earlyReduction: 0.5% per full month before normal retirement age
eligibility.note: Normal retirement: age 62 with 5+ years. Early retirement available with reduction.

contributions.note: General members: 5% employee. Employer rate set by actuarial valuation, blended 21.1% in FY2025.

cola.description: First 3% of CPI increase plus half of each percentage increase from 3% to 12%
cola.note: COLA based on U.S. Average Consumer Price Index

ssLeveling.description: Increased benefit until age 62 or SS normal retirement age, reduced benefit thereafter
ssLeveling.note: Designed to keep total monthly retirement income level when SS begins

drop.note: Deferred Retirement Option Program — up to 4 years

lumpSum.amount: lesser of 10% of actuarial value or $20,000
lumpSum.note: If actuarial value < $5,000, entire benefit may be taken as lump sum

formula.description: On retirement, member irrevocably elects benefit option. Base formula: 1.5% × AFC × Years (max 30). Members may elect between options specified in § 46-38.B. Uses creditable compensation as defined on or after 1/4/2009.

eligibility.earlyReduction: 0.5% per full month before normal retirement age

cola.description: First 3% of CPI increase plus half of each percentage increase from 3% to 12%

ssLeveling.description: Increased benefit until age 62 or SS normal retirement age, reduced benefit thereafter

lumpSum.amount: lesser of 10% of actuarial value or $20,000

formula.facNote: Average of highest 3 consecutive 12-month periods using creditable compensation as defined January 4, 2009
formula.description: 1.0% x AFC x years of creditable service (max 30 years)

applicability.employeeType: General Member NOT in a collective bargaining unit
applicability.collectiveBargainingNote: General Members who ARE in a collective bargaining unit remain on the pre-2025 formula (1.5% election options per § 46-38.A)
applicability.mapOptIn: Management Accountability Program employees hired 2012-01-12 to 2025-01-12 who only had Article IX (DC) access and opted into full Chapter 46 before January 12, 2025. Their original hire date used for vesting only.

eligibility.note: Same age/service rules as other Chapter 46 general members

contributions.note: 4% of creditable compensation + accrued vacation and comp time at retirement. Same rate as pre-2025 general members.

supplementalDC.note: County contributes 7.5% of base pay to 401(a) DC plan for post-2025 non-bargaining general employees (vs 4.2% for all other general employees). This higher DC contribution offsets the lower DB multiplier.

cola.description: First 3% of CPI increase plus half of each percentage from 3% to 12%
cola.note: Same COLA as all other Chapter 46 tiers. Based on U.S. Average CPI.

lumpSum.description: Lesser of 10% of actuarial value of lifetime allowance or $20,000

drop.note: DROP extended to 4 years per Ord. No. 23-10 (effective July 1, 2023)

acers_ch46_general_post2025.keyChange: Reduced DB multiplier (1.0% vs 1.5%) with higher DC employer contribution (7.5% vs 4.2%). Shifts retirement income structure toward DC savings.

formula.description: Public safety formula — multiplier and max service years differ from general members. Disability benefit minimum: 66⅔% of AFC.

eligibility.note: Public safety normal retirement: age 52 with 5+ years

cola.description: First 3% of CPI increase plus half of each percentage increase from 3% to 12%

ssLeveling.description: Increased benefit until age 62 or SS normal retirement age

ordinary.note: Ordinary disability after 2 years of service

lineOfDutyDeath.note: Line of duty death: 100% of § 46-38.A calculation, service requirements waived. Benefit ≥ 5% of AFC.
